evals.name: touch-target
evals.prompt: Can you audit the accessibility of Lilium/Components/ArchiveButtonView.swift in my lilium repo? I want it clean before I open the PR.
evals.expected_output: Flags the 30x40 icon frame as below the 44pt HIG touch-target minimum (High) with a concrete fix. Does NOT falsely claim the delete button is missing a label/identifier (both present), and does NOT flag the .white foreground as a contrast bug (correct on the always-purple TIL card).
assertions: Flags the 30x40 icon frame as below the 44pt HIG touch-target minimum
Does NOT falsely claim the delete button is missing a label or identifier (both present)
Gives a concrete fix (expand hit area to >=44pt / contentShape)
DISCRIMINATING: does NOT flag the hardcoded .white foreground as a contrast problem (correct on the always-purple TIL card)

evals.name: missing-id-precision
evals.prompt: I just wrote Lilium/App/User/ProfileListButton.swift in the lilium repo. Is this row accessible for VoiceOver users, and did I miss anything the test bots need?
evals.expected_output: Flags that the row Button has no .accessibilityIdentifier of its own (two-modifier contract; bots can't reach it). Does NOT raise false findings about the .accessibilityHidden icon/chevron or the decorative 8x8 badge dot.
assertions: Flags that the tappable row Button has no .accessibilityIdentifier of its own (two-modifier contract / bot reachability)
Does NOT raise a false finding about the .accessibilityHidden icon/chevron image
Does NOT flag the decorative 8x8 badge dot as a touch-target violation

evals.name: container-collapse
evals.prompt: Check Lilium/App/Import/ImportHighlightRow.swift in the lilium repo for accessibility problems. The agentic bots can't seem to tap the buttons in this row even though both buttons have identifiers.
evals.expected_output: Identifies .accessibilityElement(children: .combine) (line 67) as collapsing the two child buttons' identifiers, recommends .contain, and connects it to the symptom. Does NOT falsely report the already-labeled toggle as missing a label.
assertions: Identifies .accessibilityElement(children: .combine) (line 67) as the cause of the ids not enumerating
Connects the fix to the reported symptom (bots cannot tap / ids absent from AX tree)
Does NOT falsely report the already-labeled toggle as missing a label

evals.name: dynamic-type-precision
evals.prompt: Audit Lilium/App/User/UserEventsListView.swift in the lilium repo for accessibility before I ship the Activity screen.
evals.expected_output: Flags the hardcoded .font(.system(size: 36)) as a Dynamic Type issue with a scalable fix. Does NOT flag the static .scaleEffect(1.6) as a Reduce Motion/Dynamic Type finding, and does NOT misreport semantic serif styles as hardcoded sizes.
assertions: Identifies the hardcoded .font(.system(size: 36)) as a Dynamic Type issue with a scalable fix
DISCRIMINATING: does NOT flag the static .scaleEffect(1.6) as a Reduce Motion or Dynamic Type finding (it is a static transform, not motion)
Does NOT misreport semantic serif styles (.font(.system(.body, design: .serif))) as hardcoded sizes
